blocks.html: This how-to walks through publishing your first post with Chapbook, start to finish.
blocks.ph: Say what the reader will be able to do by the end, and roughly how long it takes. One short paragraph.

blocks.html: A GitHub account, a repository for your blog, and about ten minutes.
blocks.ph: List the kit, accounts, or starting point. If there are prerequisites, name them here so nobody gets stuck halfway.

blocks.html: First, connect your repository in Settings and pick a branch.
blocks.ph: Step 1. Replace with the first step. Write it as an instruction — one action per step.

blocks.html: Then write your post in blocks and hit Publish — Chapbook commits it for you.
blocks.ph: Step 2. Replace with the next step. Keep the order honest — write it the way you'd actually do it.

blocks.html: Keep drafts as draft:true until ready, and preview before you publish.
blocks.ph: The small things that save time or trouble — the bit you only learn by doing it once and getting it wrong.
